mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate labor income and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4%, sustained restrictive monetary policy produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days by elevating discount rates and compressing valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Months with FEDFUNDS > 4% show positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly tightens real financial conditions, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions when unemployment is below 4%, a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals a consumption slowdown that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during UNRATE below 4% periods coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply-side weakness and falling corporate revenue visibility, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative monthly INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in unemployment (UNRATE) and decreases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal a coordinated contraction in labor and goods markets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE increases and INDPRO decreases fail to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of concurrent-signal months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals forward construction activity and durable goods demand, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles with FEDFUNDS above 4%, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because restrictive policy amplifies the discount-rate impact of slowing output.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.
